headline: Protect sleep as the first recovery lever
athlete_copy: Aim for consistent bedtime habits, reduced late caffeine, and a dark cool room.
coach_copy: Keep the recovery message simple: sleep quality improves readiness, cognition, and adaptation.
practitioner_copy: Use sleep as the first intervention before adding advanced modalities.

headline: Use fueling and food quality to support recovery
athlete_copy: Prioritize consistent carbohydrates around work and include nutrient-dense recovery foods.
coach_copy: Tie nutrition support to energy, recovery, and repeatability, not body-shaming language.
practitioner_copy: Blend blood context, training phase, and behavior before recommending supplements.

headline: Progress decision-load drills intentionally
athlete_copy: Use short, sharp read-react drills and build complexity gradually.
coach_copy: Layer unpredictable cues carefully when readiness or fatigue is down.
practitioner_copy: Use cognition results to guide drill design, not to label ceiling or role fit.

headline: Interpret signals longitudinally
athlete_copy: One score never defines you. Trends are more useful than single snapshots.
coach_copy: Use trends to support conversations, not to overreact to one isolated data point.
practitioner_copy: Anchor interpretation to baselines, timing, context, and repeated measures.
